Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-27 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's data shows BE dominating the momentum rankings with a composite score of 96.9 and the #1 position, driven by extraordinary 1-year returns of 1193.72% that vastly outpace SPY by 1162.9 percentage points and QQQ by 1151.59 points. BE exhibits exceptional ratio consistency (4 of 4 ratio passes) and maintains all four major moving average gates, indicating sustained uptrend structure, while its bad_spy_score of 2.88 suggests strong resilience even on market weakness days. The runner-up STX shows more modest but still solid momentum with 617.56% 1-year returns and a 586.74 point advantage over SPY, though it scores notably lower across all three component metrics (returns, ratios, and SPY-day resilience). Both leaders pass all SMA gates and ratio consistency checks, but BE's dramatically outsized outperformance across every timeframe and benchmark represents a statistical extreme within the 700+ ticker universe being ranked.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
